world_date: Month of the Ashen Dreaming, Year 1 of the Hollow Accord, Day 19
major_event: At dawn, Rowen and Briggs descended into the submerged ruin. At midmorning, Elder Ashveil arrived at Lostfeld's outer gate and demanded entry. At noon, the Dark Council's reply reached the unit commander: hold and await further instruction. By dusk, the Shadow Court clan council had convened — and Lythara's Nythariel envoy had not arrived. All four threads reached crisis simultaneously on Day 19.

primary_event.name: Ashveil at the Gate — Entity Contact Confirmed
primary_event.summary: Elder Ashveil arrived at Lostfeld's outer gate at midmorning with two armed wardens. The Monastery relay message — 'entity contact suspected' — reached her at the foothills camp just before dawn, half an hour before she broke camp. She read it standing in the cold and said nothing to her wardens. She entered the approach road already knowing. Ironmaul met her at the outer yard, not the gate — a deliberate demotion of ceremony. She invoked Monastery override authority. Ironmaul said: 'Come and see what you are authorizing.' He took her to the garrison. Varek was awake. He looked at Ashveil and said, without greeting: 'It knows you sent someone.' She did not respond. Kessa Stonebraid stood in the corner. The stone tokens were still on the floor.

supporting_events.name: Rowen Descends — The Chamber Opens
supporting_events.summary: At first light Rowen and Briggs entered the water. The descent took forty minutes. The structure below is larger than the vellum strip suggested — a vaulted antechamber of dark stone, barnacled but intact. A door of corroded iron stands at the far end. The iron key fit. The lock turned. Rowen pushed the door open and held his sealed lantern forward. He saw shelves. He saw objects. He saw writing on the far wall in a script neither he nor Briggs could read. He took three objects that could be carried: a sealed cylinder of pale metal, a flat disc of dark glass, and a folded cloth that did not decay despite submersion. Then the lantern began to dim. They ascended. Rowen surfaced pale and silent. He went directly to his cabin and locked the door.

supporting_events.name: Shadow Court Clan Council — Lythara Exposed
supporting_events.summary: The clan council opened at dusk. Lythara's Nythariel envoy did not arrive. Velorn presented the position statement with five houses behind him. Arkenor's lieutenant observed in silence. Lythara offered the Tidefall leverage as promised — but without the Nythariel intelligence, she could only describe the Admiral's silence in general terms. Velorn interrupted: 'Our queen offers shares in a crisis she cannot name precisely.' Two of Lythara's minor client houses visibly did not speak in her defense when the floor opened. The session did not end in vote — clan councils rarely do — but the balance of visible authority shifted in the room.

supporting_events.name: Dark Council Reply — Unit Holds, Tidefall Notified
supporting_events.summary: The Dark Council's reply reached the unit commander at noon: hold until contract review completes. A separate dispatch was sent to Tidefall's admiralty notifying the Admiral that the recall order is under contract review and cannot be honored without written contract-termination from the original commissioning party. Since the Admiral is sealed in his study and the junior clerk receives the dispatch, it sits alongside Caeris's Hollow Accord letter. Neither has been read by anyone with authority. The unit is now in formal contractual hold, not merely informal non-compliance.

active_events.name: Varek and the Iron Case — Entity Speaks Through the Sealed
active_events.summary: Varek said 'It knows you sent someone' upon seeing Ashveil. He has not slept in four days. Ashveil attempted to invoke override authority. Ironmaul deferred the question. Kessa Stonebraid reported at midday that the rune-pressure on the case has intensified since Ashveil's arrival — as if proximity to a second authority figure increased whatever is pressing from within. Neither Ironmaul nor Ashveil can agree on what step comes next. The garrison soldiers have stopped speaking in the yard.
active_events.consequences: Two institutional authorities — Ironmaul's secular control and Ashveil's Monastery mandate — are now physically present and in conflict. The entity appears responsive to proximity. Varek is the only one who has direct contact with it and he is no longer clearly acting on behalf of any institution. If the case is opened, no one present knows what happens. If it is not, the pressure will continue to build.

active_events.name: Rowen — Three Objects from the Deep Chamber
active_events.summary: Rowen surfaced with three objects: a sealed pale-metal cylinder, a dark glass disc, and an undecayed folded cloth. He locked himself in his cabin. Briggs told the crew only that they found a room and that the captain was thinking. The navigator has not been called. The fleet holds anchor. Rowen has not emerged by evening. The cloth, when he unfolded it in the dim lamplight of his cabin, showed a map — not of the island, but of a coastline he did not recognize.
active_events.consequences: Rowen now holds objects of unknown provenance from a sealed chamber that predates the island's submergence. The cloth-map is the most immediately actionable object — it may point to something reachable. The pale cylinder may be a container, the dark glass disc may be an instrument. None of these can be identified without expertise Rowen's crew does not have. His next move will define whether this becomes a quest or a burden.

active_events.name: The Recall Standoff — Contract Review Holds the Camp
active_events.summary: The Dark Council reply has transformed the situation from informal non-compliance to formal contractual hold. The unit sits at four miles. The Tidefall dispatch notifying the Admiral of the review sits unread beside Caeris's Hollow Accord letter. Caeris learned this morning that the camp still stands and that no reply had come from Tidefall. He visited the admiralty office at midday on an unrelated pretext and saw the stack of unread documents on the desk. He did not take them. He left.
active_events.consequences: Caeris now knows the admiralty desk is unattended and that the Tidefall governance structure is functionally absent. He is one decision away from determining whether to exploit this gap or wait for legitimate authority to re-emerge. The twin loyalist nobles dine with him tonight. The two-signature contingency clause is on the table.

active_events.name: The Proclamation Crisis — Nobles Dine with Caeris
active_events.summary: The two loyalist nobles arrived for the private dinner. Caeris presented the legal case: the contingency clause exists, Roderic cannot be counted on for public appearance, the Dur Khadur camp will not move without resolution, and every day without announcement is a day Tidefall's absence becomes precedent for ignoring the Hollow Accord entirely. One noble, Lord Halveth, agreed to sign conditionally — contingent on a third signature from a senior clergy witness. The second, Lady Orryn, asked for one more day.
active_events.consequences: Caeris is one conditional signature and one undecided noble away from the two he needs. The clergy witness requirement from Halveth introduces a new actor. Lady Orryn's request for delay is a soft yes or a soft no — unclear. The king did not wake today. The physician sent no message, which is its own message.

active_events.name: Shadow Court — The Council's Fracture Visible
active_events.summary: The council session ended without formal vote but with visible damage. Two of Lythara's minor client houses did not speak in her defense. Velorn's position statement is now entered into the clan record. Arkenor's lieutenant departed without committing to either side. Lythara returned to her quarters and found the Nythariel envoy had arrived — two hours after the session began. The intelligence is in her hands but the moment is gone.
active_events.consequences: Lythara has the Tidefall intelligence now, one session too late. She may call a follow-up session to reassert control, but calling a second council so quickly signals desperation. Velorn knows this. Arkenor's neutrality is now the swing position. Whoever reaches Arkenor first with a compelling offer shapes the Court's near future.

active_events.name: The Admiral's Sealed Study — Document Identity Suspected
active_events.summary: The senior trade councillor received a private note from the Vampire operative via a third-party intermediary: the sealed document was logged as originating from an external courier of unspecified origin. The councillor now suspects the document is a foreign instrument — possibly from the Shadow Court or from an actor connected to the succession crisis in the Twin Cities. He shared this with his two council allies. A motion to formally request the Admiral's emergence is being drafted.
active_events.consequences: The councillor's suspicion about foreign origin is directionally correct but not specific. He does not know it is connected to the succession crisis. If the motion passes, the Admiral will face formal institutional pressure to emerge or be declared temporarily incapacitated, which triggers the electorate succession protocols.

active_events.name: Vilefin Tinker — Unsold Details, Approaching Farrock
active_events.summary: The tinker-informant reached the outer approaches of Farrock by late morning. He requested entry through the trade gate with a standard merchant identification. He has not yet decided to sell his remaining details — the sound Varek made at night and the smell of cold iron beneath the garrison floor. He is watching how Farrock receives him before deciding what he is worth to them.
active_events.consequences: If Farrock's garrison commander shows interest in Lostfeld intelligence, the tinker has a product of unusual specificity. If Farrock seems indifferent or hostile, the tinker will hold the details for a different buyer — possibly the Gnomes on his return south.

active_events.name: Glenhaven Intelligence — Wayfarer Reaches Twin Cities
active_events.summary: The Glenhaven Wayfarer bearing Elowen's facilitation offer arrived at the Twin Cities gate this morning and requested a meeting with the regent-designate. Caeris received him in the early afternoon — briefly, between the admiralty visit and dinner preparation. The Wayfarer presented Glenhaven's offer: a neutral facilitation channel, quiet Wayfarer observation presence, and access to the northern intelligence network in exchange for the Twin Cities' formal endorsement of the Wayfarers' protected-messenger status under the Hollow Accord.
active_events.consequences: This is Elowen's calculated play — she offers Caeris external legitimacy and intelligence access at a moment when he is isolated. If Caeris accepts, Glenhaven becomes formally embedded in the succession process. If he declines, Elowen learns exactly how desperate or how confident he is.

faction_actions.action: Ironmaul escorted Ashveil to the garrison personally rather than delegate, denying her privacy with Varek. When Varek addressed Ashveil, Ironmaul watched her face. He posted Kessa Stonebraid as permanent observer in the garrison room. He did not eject Ashveil — her authority is not yet settled — but he set the terms: she may observe, she may speak to Varek, she may not touch the case or instruct the garrison without his countersignature. Cost: 3 gold (doubled garrison upkeep, rune-warden observation), 2 influence.
faction_actions.reason: Ironmaul is threading two competing institutional claims. He cannot throw out a Monastery Elder without triggering a crisis with Stonebreak. He can contain her inside a procedural framework. The case is the priority — he will sacrifice the political relationship if Ashveil endangers his control of it.
faction_actions.target: Elder Ashveil — constrained to observer status; Kessa Stonebraid — posted as permanent observer; garrison — doubled and locked

faction_actions.action: Ashveil invoked override authority at the gate and again in the garrison. She did not immediately challenge Ironmaul's observer restriction — she calculated that a direct confrontation over procedure would lose her access entirely. She spent the afternoon observing Varek. At dusk she composed a priority letter to Warden Aldric: 'Varek is communicating with the entity. The case was built to contain something that was already present. Ironmaul controls access. I need Stonebreak to formally escalate to the Thane's Council, not to Ironmaul directly.' Cost: 3 gold (travel completion, priority dispatch), 2 influence.
faction_actions.reason: Ashveil has assessed the situation accurately: she cannot win a direct confrontation. She is escalating past Ironmaul to the institutional layer above him. The Thane's Council is a dwarven body — if Stonebreak can convince it to intervene, Ironmaul loses his singular control.
faction_actions.target: Warden Aldric — priority escalation letter dispatched; Ironmaul — procedural deference maintained while escalation route activates

faction_actions.action: The Dark Council completed the contract review. Finding: the original commission for the Twin Cities deployment was signed by the Twin Cities regent-designate office, not by Tidefall. Tidefall has no formal standing to recall a unit contracted to a separate party. The Council dispatched a second letter to the unit: hold until the Twin Cities regent-designate issues a formal stand-down, or until the Council terminates the contract on its own initiative. Cost: 2 gold (dispatch, unit upkeep), 2 military (continued deployment pressure).
faction_actions.reason: The Dark Council has found the contractual loophole that lets them justify continued presence without appearing to defy a legitimate order. They are not at war with Tidefall — they are enforcing contract terms. This is the Dark Council at its most characteristic: legal precision as a weapon.
faction_actions.target: Unit commander — hold order confirmed with contractual basis; Tidefall — formal notification of contract review result dispatched; Twin Cities — implicit pressure on Caeris to resolve

faction_actions.action: The senior trade councillor's formal emergence motion gathered a third signature and was formally submitted to the admiralty clerk as an official council inquiry. It requests that the Admiral confirm his fitness for governance by appearing at the council table within two days or submitting a written statement. The motion is now official institutional record. The prophet was informed by a page and spent the evening in agitated prayer. Cost: 2 gold (administrative), 3 influence (institutional pressure).
faction_actions.reason: The councillors have enough signatures and enough alarm. The foreign-origin document detail has made this politically safe — they are not attacking the Admiral, they are protecting the institution from foreign interference. That framing gives them cover.
faction_actions.target: Admiral — formal emergence request submitted; prophet — informed; admiralty clerk — motion now on record

faction_actions.action: Caeris heard the Glenhaven Wayfarer's offer and said he would give a formal reply within two days. Then he hosted the private dinner. Lord Halveth's conditional yes and Lady Orryn's delay request are now his working materials. After the nobles departed, Caeris sent a quiet inquiry to the Twin Cities' senior clergy house: would the High Cleric witness a proclamation signature under the contingency clause? He did not specify a date. Cost: 3 gold (dinner, clergy inquiry, Wayfarer reception), 3 influence.
faction_actions.reason: Caeris is threading four simultaneous preparations: Dur Khadur standoff, Hollow Accord diplomacy, Glenhaven facilitation offer, and the succession announcement. He is not acting yet on any of them, but he is ensuring none of them collapses before he is ready to move.
faction_actions.target: High Cleric — witness inquiry sent; Lord Halveth, Lady Orryn — dinner relationship deepened; Glenhaven Wayfarer — two-day reply window granted

faction_actions.action: Lythara read the Nythariel intelligence at midnight. She now knows: the sealed document delivered to the Admiral came from an external courier, the Admiral has not emerged in three days, and the trade councillors are moving toward a fitness inquiry. She spent an hour deciding and then summoned her senior intelligence warden. She will request a private meeting with Arkenor's house — not through the formal council channel but through a personal envoy. If Arkenor can be moved to her side, the council fracture becomes manageable. Cost: 4 gold (envoy preparation, intelligence handling), 3 influence.
faction_actions.reason: Lythara knows she lost the council session but not the war. Arkenor is the swing position. She has the Tidefall intelligence now and can make a more specific offer than she could yesterday. She is moving fast before Velorn reaches Arkenor first.
faction_actions.target: Arkenor — personal envoy dispatched; council damage — containment strategy activated

faction_actions.action: The Gnome pass relay station logged three new messages today: Ashveil's priority dispatch to Aldric, a Farrock inquiry about northern routes, and a request from an unknown party for intelligence on the Sinking Island's current anchorage location. The Gnomes flagged the Sinking Island request — it is unusual and could be valuable or dangerous — and held it pending internal review. They did not deliver it. The Farrock inquiry was answered with standard routing information. Cost: 1 gold (relay operations), 1 influence.
faction_actions.reason: The Sinking Island anchorage request is anomalous. Someone knows Rowen is there and wants to know exactly where. The Gnomes do not deliver intelligence about active naval positions without understanding who is asking and why — that is how they stay indispensable and alive.
faction_actions.target: Ashveil dispatch — forwarded to Stonebreak; Farrock inquiry — answered; Sinking Island anchorage request — held for review

faction_actions.action: Elowen received confirmation that her Wayfarer had delivered the facilitation offer and that Caeris had given a two-day reply window. She assessed this as positive — a rejection would have been immediate. She ordered two additional Wayfarers to stand by near the Twin Cities border, not to enter, but to be available if the offer was accepted. She also reviewed the Lostfeld partial intelligence again and noted that Ashveil's arrival was imminent — the picture was escalating faster than she anticipated. Cost: 2 gold (Wayfarer deployment, intelligence review), 1 influence.
faction_actions.reason: Elowen is a careful actor. She does not crowd the offer — she positions resources for rapid follow-through if yes comes back. The Lostfeld situation concerns her because it involves the Monastery, which is close enough to Stonebreak to affect the Gnome pass network she depends on.
faction_actions.target: Twin Cities border — two Wayfarers on standby; Caeris offer — two-day window monitored

faction_actions.action: Rowen spent the day in his cabin with the three objects. By afternoon he had made one determination: the cloth-map shows a coastline with five marked points, one of which matches a known island chain in the southern Dreadwind approach — his own waters. The others are unfamiliar. He called Briggs in at dusk and showed him the map without context. Briggs identified the same matching island. Rowen locked the map away and told Briggs: 'You saw a map. That is all you saw.' He sent no message to his fleet command. Cost: 1 gold (cabin isolation, crew management), 1 influence.
faction_actions.reason: Rowen is processing. He does not share strategic discoveries until he has a use for them. The Dreadwind match on the map is either confirmation he is on the right path home or a trap. He needs to think before he moves.
faction_actions.target: Three objects — secured in cabin; Briggs — instructed to silence; fleet — holds anchor, no new orders

faction_actions.action: The tinker-informant entered Farrock through the trade gate and was assigned a merchant bunk. He made casual conversation with a garrison quartermaster over dinner and learned: Farrock is actively anxious about Lostfeld, has received vague reassurances from the Twin Cities that it considers insufficient, and is watching the northern routes nervously. The tinker decided his remaining details are worth selling here. He requested a meeting with the garrison intelligence officer for tomorrow morning. Cost: 1 gold (gate fee, bunk), 0 influence.
faction_actions.reason: Farrock's anxiety is the tinker's market. He now knows his product has a willing buyer. He will calibrate his price based on how the intelligence officer receives him tomorrow.
faction_actions.target: Farrock garrison intelligence officer — meeting requested for morning; unsold Lostfeld details — prepared for sale

faction_actions.action: Farrock's garrison commander reviewed the seven-day watchtower log audit. Two anomalies: a signal on Day 13 that was logged as 'weather interference' but matches the pattern of a delayed acknowledgment code, and a gap on Day 16 in the northern signal chain suggesting someone or something passed through a monitored approach without triggering the expected relay. He brought both to the intelligence officer, who confirmed the Day 16 gap aligns with the direction of Lostfeld. Tomorrow morning a Vilefin merchant has requested an intelligence meeting. Cost: 2 gold (audit completion, officer briefing), 1 influence.
faction_actions.reason: Farrock is a fortress that survives by knowing what is in its approaches. The Day 16 gap is more alarming than the Day 13 anomaly — something moved through the northern corridor without signaling. The Vilefin meeting tomorrow may clarify it.
faction_actions.target: Watchtower log anomalies — two flagged; intelligence officer — briefed; Vilefin merchant meeting — accepted for morning

faction_actions.action: The operative received confirmation that the intelligence about the document's external origin had reached the trade councillor and was being used to build the emergence motion. The operative sent a brief coded update to Lythara's network: 'Motion filed. Admiral under institutional pressure. Two days to emergence or incapacitation ruling.' The operative also noted, for internal records only, that the document's actual origin remained unconfirmed — they only knew it was external. Cost: 2 gold (network relay), 2 influence.
faction_actions.reason: The Vampires are playing the long network game. They delivered actionable intelligence and watched it work. They do not yet know who sent the document — that is the piece they need next, because whoever sent it has leverage over the Admiral, and that leverage is more valuable than the intelligence itself.
faction_actions.target: Lythara network — emergence motion status relayed; document origin — marked as unresolved priority

faction_actions.action: Elder Mrak's rerouting proposal came to a second informal discussion. The caravan suspension has now lasted long enough that three crew chiefs have sent formal complaints about crew deterioration — the opioid-dependent teams are becoming erratic without the rhythm of the road. The elder council authorized a test route: one caravan through the northern bypass, avoiding the Twin Cities south road entirely, heading toward Frostvale via the Rock Plains eastern track. Cost: 2 gold (rerouting preparation, crew management), 2 military (unpredictable crew risk).
faction_actions.reason: The council is not solving the addiction problem — they are routing around the crisis point. The northern bypass through the Rock Plains puts their caravans in Gnome and Vilefin territory, which has its own risks. But the crews are deteriorating and doing nothing is no longer viable.
faction_actions.target: Test caravan — northern bypass route authorized; elder council — rerouting debate continues; opioid crew deterioration — deferred not addressed

faction_actions.action: After the nobles departed, Caeris sat alone with the proclamation's sealed document and the Glenhaven Wayfarer's offer written beside it. He drafted one page: a summary of the present crisis as he understood it, with four columns — Dur Khadur, Tidefall, Glenhaven, Proclamation — and the dependencies between them. He folded it, did not address it to anyone, and placed it inside the archive beside the proclamation. He extinguished his lamp. He slept for the first time in two days. Cost: 1 gold (materials), 2 influence.
faction_actions.reason: Caeris is the most rational actor in a field of deteriorating institutions. He is not acting — he is mapping. The document he created tonight is a decision tree. Tomorrow he will begin to execute it.
faction_actions.target: Personal strategy document — created; proclamation — still sealed; all four active pressures — mapped and held

recent_events.text: Elder Ashveil arrived at Lostfeld's outer gate at midmorning. Ironmaul escorted her to the garrison. Varek addressed her immediately: 'It knows you sent someone.' Kessa Stonebraid reported that rune-pressure on the iron case intensified after Ashveil's arrival. Ironmaul restricted Ashveil to observer status. She accepted the restriction and spent the afternoon composing an escalation letter to Stonebreak.

Rowen and Briggs completed the dawn dive. They entered the submerged chamber, opened the sealed door with the iron key, and recovered three objects: a pale-metal cylinder, a dark glass disc, and a folded cloth map. Rowen surfaced pale and silent, locked himself in his cabin, and spent the day in isolation. By dusk he had identified one of the five map points as matching a known Dreadwind island chain.

The clan council convened at dusk. Lythara's Nythariel envoy had not arrived. Velorn presented his position statement with five houses backing him. Two of Lythara's client houses stayed silent when the floor opened. No vote was taken, but the visible authority balance shifted. The envoy arrived two hours into the session — too late.

The Glenhaven Wayfarer delivered Elowen's facilitation offer to Caeris. Lord Halveth gave a conditional yes to the proclamation signature; Lady Orryn asked for one more day. Caeris sent a quiet inquiry to the High Cleric about witnessing the announcement. After all guests departed, he drafted a four-column decision map and slept.

The senior trade councillor's formal emergence motion gathered three signatures and was submitted to the admiralty clerk. The Admiral has two days to appear or submit a written statement before incapacitation protocols engage. The Dur Khadur contract-review dispatch sits unread alongside Caeris's Hollow Accord letter on the unattended admiralty desk.

active_tensions.factions: Lostfeld — Ironmaul vs Ashveil vs Entity (Three-Way Standoff)
active_tensions.description: Ironmaul controls access. Ashveil has invoked override authority and been procedurally contained. Varek appears to be in active communication with whatever is in or pressing against the case. Kessa Stonebraid's rune readings suggest the entity is aware of and responsive to institutional authority. Ashveil has escalated to the Thane's Council — Ironmaul does not yet know this.

active_tensions.factions: Twin Cities — Caeris, Four Active Pressures Converging
active_tensions.description: Dur Khadur holds contractually at four miles. Tidefall is governmentally absent. Glenhaven has made a facilitation offer with a two-day window. The proclamation requires two noble signatures and a clergy witness. All four pressures will demand resolution simultaneously within the next two days.

active_tensions.factions: Shadow Court — Arkenor as Swing Position
active_tensions.description: Both Lythara and Velorn are moving toward Arkenor. Lythara has dispatched a personal envoy with the now-complete Tidefall intelligence. Velorn has a one-session lead in the public record. Arkenor's lieutenant departed the council without committing. Whoever reaches Arkenor with the better offer in the next tick shapes the Court's alignment.

The formal emergence motion is on record. The Admiral has two days. The prophet is in the corridor. The sealed document's origin is still unknown to the councillors but suspected foreign. The Dur Khadur contract dispute has added an external legitimacy crisis to an already-fractured internal one.

seer_state.belief_about_user: The presence beyond the veil is not fully understood.
